audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches [[grammar:hoeven niet te + infinitive]] and [[grammar:mogen + infinitive]]. Use [[grammar:hoeven niet te]] for do not have to. Use [[grammar:mogen]] to say that an action is allowed.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter introduces [[grammar:hoeven niet te + infinitive]]: Hoef/noeven is licensed under negation and selects a te-infinitive complement.; [[grammar:mogen + infinitive]]: The finite modal [[grammar:mogen]] carries agreement while the lexical infinitive closes the clause.

audienceSections.normal:
#### hoeven niet te + infinitive

Use [[grammar:hoeven niet te]] for do not have to. Example: [[grammar:Je hoeft het boek vandaag niet terug te brengen.]]

#### mogen + infinitive

Use [[grammar:mogen]] to say that an action is allowed. Example: [[grammar:In de stiltezone mag je alleen zacht praten.]]
audienceSections.expert:
#### hoeven niet te + infinitive

Hoef/noeven is licensed under negation and selects a te-infinitive complement. Literal example: [[grammar:Je hoeft het boek vandaag niet terug te brengen.]]

#### mogen + infinitive

The finite modal [[grammar:mogen]] carries agreement while the lexical infinitive closes the clause. Literal example: [[grammar:In de stiltezone mag je alleen zacht praten.]]

audienceSections.normal: [[grammar:Hoeven niet te]] removes necessity. [[grammar:Mogen]] grants permission; the action infinitive appears at the end.
audienceSections.expert: [[grammar:Hoeven niet te]] removes necessity. [[grammar:Mogen]] grants permission; the action infinitive appears at the end.

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:Oma Anja, wat is de belangrijkste regel hier?]] — Grandma Anja, what is the most important rule here?
- [[grammar:In de stiltezone mag je alleen zacht praten.]] — In the quiet zone you may only speak softly.
- [[grammar:Mag ik met mijn telefoon een foto maken?]] — May I take a photo with my phone?
- [[grammar:Ja, maar je hoeft daarvoor geen toestemming te vragen.]] — Yes, but you do not have to ask permission for that.
- [[grammar:Hoe lang is de uitleentermijn?]] — How long is the loan period?
- [[grammar:Je mag het boek drie weken houden.]] — You may keep the book for three weeks.
- [[grammar:Moet ik het daarna meteen terugbrengen?]] — Do I have to return it immediately after that?
- [[grammar:Je hoeft het boek vandaag niet terug te brengen.]] — You do not have to return the book today.
- [[grammar:Kan ik de uitleentermijn verlengen?]] — Can I renew the loan period?
- [[grammar:Ja, dat mag online met je pas.]] — Yes, that is allowed online with your card.
- [[grammar:Wanneer krijg je een boete?]] — When do you receive a fine?
- [[grammar:Alleen wanneer het boek te laat terugkomt.]] — Only when the book is returned too late.
- [[grammar:Er staan zeshonderdtwintig boeken in deze afdeling.]] — There are six hundred twenty books in this section.
- [[grammar:Met je pas mag je zes boeken lenen.]] — With your card you may borrow six books.
- [[grammar:Ik hoef dus niet alles vandaag te kiezen.]] — So I do not have to choose everything today.
- [[grammar:Precies. Neem rustig de tijd.]] — Exactly. Take your time.
- [[grammar:Dan fluister ik verder in de stiltezone.]] — Then I will continue whispering in the quiet zone.
- [[grammar:En ik verleng mijn boek thuis.]] — And I will renew my book at home.
